title: GenAI Chatbot (RAG)
subtitle: Document Q&A with semantic search — personal production-style build
summary: AI-powered chatbot with RAG for document Q&A and semantic search. Public repo exploring retrieval, grounding, and chat UX patterns I also use in enterprise work.
problem: Teams need answers from their own documents — not generic chat that hallucinates policy or process.
motivation: I wanted a clean personal sandbox for RAG: ingest → embed → retrieve → answer, so enterprise lessons stayed sharp outside office codebases.
architecture: Python RAG pipeline with document ingestion, embeddings, vector retrieval, and a chat interface for grounded Q&A. Designed so category/document management and evaluation can grow without rewriting the core loop.
challenges: Keeping answers faithful to retrieved chunks
Balancing recall vs precision in semantic search
Making the chat path understandable for non-ML users
performance: Focused on grounded responses rather than open-ended generation
lessons: Operator-facing controls matter even in personal tools

title: AI Mock Interviewer
subtitle: Resume-aware practice rounds with feedback and scoring
summary: Upload a résumé, practice personalized interview rounds, and get instant feedback — a personal product experiment in applied LLMs for career prep.
problem: Interview prep is generic. Candidates need questions shaped by their actual résumé and role target.
motivation: Apply conversational AI beyond internal ops — help people rehearse with structure and scoring.
architecture: Python app that ingests résumé context, generates role-aware interview rounds, and returns feedback/scoring after responses.
challenges: Personalizing questions without leaking irrelevant noise
Useful feedback that is specific, not vague praise
lessons: Product framing matters as much as the model call

title: Wealth Intelligence + AI Chat
subtitle: Account Aggregator dashboard with grounded financial Q&A
summary: At Parag Parikh: full-stack wealth dashboard over AA APIs, unified multi-asset views, and a RAG chatbot (LangChain, FAISS, Qwen-13B) for relationship managers.
problem: Client wealth data lived across statements and asset classes. RMs needed consent-safe aggregation and natural-language answers without manual spreadsheet archaeology.
motivation: Bring Account Aggregator data and conversational AI into one calm operational surface.
architecture: Next.js/React client + FastAPI services for AA consent/token flows and normalization. Holdings across MF, equities, FDs, insurance surface in graphs and drill-downs. RAG over live client context via LangChain + FAISS + Qwen-13B for questions like net worth or fund exposure. MySQL + Docker for deployable services.
challenges: Secure consent and token lifecycle with AA APIs
Normalizing heterogeneous AA payloads into reliable insights
Keeping chatbot answers data-grounded for financial conversations
performance: Unified multi-asset views for RM workflows
Instant grounded responses for common portfolio questions
lessons: In finance, auditability and consent are product features
RAG must cite the portfolio truth, not approximate it
future: Broader evaluation on RM question sets
Deeper alerting on portfolio change narratives

title: Ticketing Platform + RAG Chat
subtitle: Fine-tuned 7B assistant for document-grounded support
summary: At L&T PESIc: production ticketing with embedded RAG chatbot, FAISS retrieval, WebSocket chat, admin document management, plus ML for resolution-time prediction.
problem: Support teams needed faster, document-aware answers inside the ticketing flow — not a separate knowledge wiki nobody opens.
motivation: Put RAG where the work already happens: tickets, live chat, and admin-managed corpora.
architecture: FastAPI services, fine-tuned 7B LLM, FAISS category-based retrieval, WebSocket live chat with async concurrency, admin panel for categories/documents, ingestion for multiple document types. Companion ML models (Random Forest / Linear Regression) estimate resolution time.
challenges: Stable retrieval across categories under concurrent chat
Feedback loops that actually improve the model over time
performance: Real-time WebSocket chat for concurrent users
lessons: Fine-tuning helps when the domain language is sticky
Prediction models build trust when wait times are visible
future: Tighter coupling between feedback and retraining cadence
